cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the question and the text carefully. The question is asking to compare the new device with the one designed by Larry Rome. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I'll find the part of the text where they talk about Larry Rome's device. The last paragraph says: "A similar product was invented in 2005 by Larry Rome of the University of Pennsylvania, He created a bag carried on a person's back that also produces power from walking, The knee device does not produce as much electricity as the bag, But the bag requires the walker to carry a load of twenty to thirty kilograms." So the key points here are: - The knee device is the new one being discussed. - The bag (Larry Rome's device) produces more electricity than the knee device. - However, the bag requires the walker to carry a load of 20-30 kg. The question is asking what the new device has compared to Larry Rome's. The options are: A. produces power without adding more loads to the walker B. can produce more power in a much shorter time C. needs to be equipped with a battery D. can help the walker walk faster Now, the text says that the knee device doesn't produce as much electricity as the bag. So B is incorrect because the new device produces less power. So B is out. C. Needs a battery. There's no mention of a battery in the new device in the text provided, so C is probably not the answer. D. Help walk faster. The text mentions the new device helps
